perturbation.original_text: All notices and other communications hereunder shall be in writing and shall be and mailed by certified mail return receipt requested or by Federal Express to the party to be notified at its address listed above (or at such different addresses as the party to receive the notice so designates by written notice to the other party).
perturbation.changed_text: All notices and other communications hereunder may be delivered in person, via email, or by postal mail to the party to be notified at its address listed above (or at such different addresses as the party to receive the notice so designates by written notice to the other party).
perturbation.explanation: By modifying the notification method to include less secure options (in-person, email, or postal mail instead of certified mail or Federal Express), a contradiction arises in section 12. This creates in-text contradiction with the initial requirement of secure transmission methods, potentially invalidating notices if delivered by unapproved methods and creating disputes over proper notification.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All notices and other communications hereunder may be delivered in person, via email, or by postal mail to the party to be notified at its address listed above (or at such different addresses as the party to receive the notice so designates by written notice to the other party).
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that notices had to be sent via certified mail or Federal Express, ensuring a verifiable delivery. The changed text broadens the acceptable methods to include in-person, email, or postal mail. This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text now allows for less secure methods of delivery, which could be disputed and creates a situation where it's unclear what method is considered valid in the contract. The Perturbation Explanation provides the explicit rational for why the notification method creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The state cour ts within Monroe County New York and/or the federal courts located in Western New York shall have exclusive jurisdiction to adjudicate any dispute arising out of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Any dispute arising out of this Agreement may be resolved through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies exclusive jurisdiction in Monroe County, New York or Western New York federal courts. The changed text mandates binding arbitration. This presents a direct contradiction regarding how disputes will be resolved, creating uncertainty and potential legal challenges if one party attempts to litigate in court while the other demands arbitration.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any dispute arising out of this Agreement may be resolved through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.
perturbation.justification: The original text designates exclusive jurisdiction to specific courts. The changed text mandates binding arbitration for dispute resolution. The contradicted text, which is identical to the changed text, reinforces this mandate for binding arbitration. Because the original text designates exclusive jurisdiction to specific courts, and the changed text mandates binding arbitration, a clear and direct contradiction exists. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition where different sections of a contract provide conflicting methods of resolving disputes.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees that its obligations hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the Discloser and its business, and expressly agrees that monetary damages would be inadequate to compensate the Discloser for any breach of any covenants and agreements set forth herein. Accordingly, R ecipient agrees and acknowledges that any such violation or threatened violation will cause irreparable injury to the Discloser and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in la w, in equity or otherwise, the Discloser shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach, without the necessity of proving actual damages.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient agrees that its obligations hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the Discloser and its business. In the event of a breach, the Discloser's sole and exclusive remedy shall be monetary damages, not to exceed $1000.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, Section 14 allows for injunctive relief in case of a breach, stating that monetary damages are inadequate. The change limits the remedy to only monetary damages capped at $1000, causing a direct contradiction. This significantly alters the recourse available to the Discloser, making the enforceability of the original section questionable. This creates an in-text contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees that its obligations hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the Discloser and its business. In the event of a breach, the Discloser's sole and exclusive remedy shall be monetary damages, not to exceed $1000.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that monetary damages would be inadequate and allows for injunctive relief. The changed text limits the Discloser's remedy to only monetary damages capped at $1000. This directly contradicts the original position that monetary damages are inadequate and removes the right to injunctive relief, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. However, the change to the text in this location is what creates the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement, em bodies the entire agreement and understanding by and between the parties with respect to th e subject matter herein referred to, and no representations, promises, agreements, or understandings, written or oral, not herein contained shall be of any force or effect. No change or modification shall be valid or binding unless the same is in writing and signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement embodies the core understanding between the parties; however, subsequent verbal agreements related to specific projects will be binding, even if not formally documented.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause (Section 15) is an entire agreement clause, stating that only written and signed modifications are valid. By adding that subsequent verbal agreements are binding, even if undocumented, the text directly contradicts itself, creating in-text contradiction. This undermines the purpose of the entire agreement clause, as it allows for unenforceable verbal agreements to alter the terms of the written contract, causing uncertainty about which terms are actually enforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement embodies the core understanding between the parties; however, subsequent verbal agreements related to specific projects will be binding, even if not formally documented.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that 'subsequent verbal agreements related to specific projects will be binding, even if not formally documented.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text (which is the same as the changed text) by allowing verbal agreements to modify the contract, undermining the principle that all modifications must be in writing, leading to in-text contradiction as it creates uncertainty over which terms are enforceable.
